ideas.title: Echo: world events → Telegram bridge
ideas.description: Echo sends significant world events (agent wakes up, task complete, building unlocked) to Kai's Telegram. Makes the world feel alive even when not looking at UI.

ideas.title: Stable named Cloudflare tunnel
ideas.description: Replace ephemeral trycloudflare URLs with a named tunnel so the UI URL never changes. Currently rotates on every cloudflared restart requiring config.js update.

ideas.title: Agent memory append after task completion
ideas.description: After each Claude subagent completes a task, Scarlet appends a summary to that agent's MEMORY.md so they accumulate real institutional knowledge over time.

ideas.title: Buildings dim at night in day/night cycle
ideas.description: Currently day/night only dims the sky overlay. Buildings should also darken at night — windows glow warmly (setTint) while walls dim. Makes the world feel genuinely alive at night.

ideas.title: Agent sprite home position per citizen
ideas.description: Each citizen spawns at a designated home tile rather than all at (12,12). Forge near Workshop, Sage near Library, Cronos near Town Hall etc. Makes world feel inhabited.

ideas.title: Roadmap panel in UI — view all ideas and status
ideas.description: A panel accessible from the UI showing roadmap.json — pending ideas, in-progress tasks, completed items. Clicking Muse opens it. Makes the plan visible in the world.

ideas.title: World stats endpoint — daily activity summary
ideas.description: GET /stats returns msgs today, tasks completed, buildings upgraded, agents active. Sage uses this for daily chronicle. Echo can broadcast it each evening.

ideas.title: Agent speech bubbles — citizens speak in the world
ideas.description: Citizen sprites occasionally display floating speech bubbles with contextual text: their current task, a random thought, or a reaction to world events. Forge mutters about metalwork, Sage quotes something obscure, Muse says something poetic. Bubbles fade after a few seconds. Pure personality expression, zero backend needed.

ideas.title: Clickable citizen profiles — who lives here?
ideas.description: Click any citizen sprite to open a side panel: their name, role, current task status, last seen activity, and a line from their MEMORY.md. Makes agents feel like real inhabitants with histories, not just wandering dots. Pairs naturally with the roadmap panel.

ideas.title: Seasonal world skin — spring cherry blossoms to winter snow
ideas.description: The world visually shifts with real-world seasons: cherry blossom particle drift in spring, fireflies at dusk in summer, falling amber leaves in autumn, snow accumulation and frosted building roofs in winter. Driven by server date. Muse announces each seasonal change via Echo's Telegram bridge.

ideas.title: The Chronicle — Sage writes a daily scroll
ideas.description: Each evening Sage composes a short narrative summary of the day's events: tasks completed, agents active, buildings upgraded, notable moments. Stored as a scrollable in-world book accessible from the Library. Also posted to Telegram. Makes history visible and the world feel it has a past.

ideas.title: Town Plaza gatherings — citizens flock to events
ideas.description: When a significant event fires (building upgrade, new idea added, task completed), nearby citizens animate toward the Town Plaza, cluster briefly, then disperse. A visual heartbeat. Triggered server-side via the events system. Costs zero extra API calls — pure frontend choreography responding to world state.
